---
title: "LRPITT13b: SES-adjusted modified-ITT offer effect on letter-sound knowledge (L)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPITT13B is the **SES-adjusted available-case modified-ITT** analysis for letter-sound knowledge (YARC-LSK) (`yarclet`)—the SES-robustness companion to LRPITT07. It uses the uniform specification plus linear adjustment for parental education (`MUMEDUPOST16`, `DADEDUPOST16`) and age first exposed to books (`AGEBOOKS`). Requesting these covariates imposes an additional SES complete-case restriction beyond the outcome-specific observed-data requirement; LRPITT14b is the matched unadjusted comparator on exactly those rows, so LRPITT13B versus LRPITT14b isolates adjustment with the selected sample held fixed. The estimand is the randomised offer effect on $L_{\text{post}}$ in that SES-complete fitted sample, not a full-population effect for all 57 randomised children.

_Identification and sign convention._ Under the locked causal DAG, $G$ is a randomised root, so randomisation identifies the offer contrast $\tau$ in the SES-complete fitted sample without adjustment. Extending it beyond that selected observed-data sample requires ignorable exclusion and missingness. The own baseline, linear age, and the SES adjusters, are
**precision terms only** — they sharpen $\tau$ but are not what licenses the causal
interpretation. **Sign convention:** $G = 1$ is the intervention arm, so a
**positive** $\tau$ means the intervention raises the outcome.

## Model

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \tau\, G_i
       + \gamma_{\text{own}}\, \text{logit}_*(L_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
       + \gamma_A\, A_{\text{std},i} + \sum_{c \in \text{SES}} \gamma_c\, z(c_i),
$$

with Beta-Binomial likelihood $L_{\text{post},i} \sim \text{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu_i\kappa, (1-\mu_i)\kappa)$,
$\mu_i = \sigma(\eta_i)$, $N = 32$. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.
The SES adjusters enter as linear $\gamma_c$ terms (the same weak $\text{Normal}(0, 0.3)$ prior as cross-baseline couplings); HSGPs are off by default.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_itt.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
